mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate, directly boosting equity valuations and yielding positive 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 4.5% within a single month, it signals a rapid labor market deterioration that triggers recession fears, compressing equity multiples and driving negative 5-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises above 4.5% in a single month
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in industrial production and consumer credit indicate a synchronized demand-supply shock that erodes corporate profit margins, predicting negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active rate hikes, rising core inflation expectations force the Fed to maintain restrictive policy longer than markets price in, causing negative 20-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns in tightening periods do not differ significantly from baseline, or the hypothesis predicts positive returns when CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS both rise.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in real GDP indicates expanding economic activity that supports corporate earnings, generating positive 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns in contraction quarters are not significantly lower than expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When nonfarm payrolls fall below 100,000 for two consecutive months, it signals a sharp deceleration in household income growth, reducing consumption demand and driving negative 20-day forward returns.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS monthly change falls below 100.0 for two consecutive months
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where housing starts fall while industrial production rises indicates a sectoral rebalancing that typically precedes broader economic slowdowns, predicting negative 5-day forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict returns with statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes insignificant in a rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-inflation environment, rising unemployment forces the Fed to pivot aggressively to support employment, which initially boosts equity valuations but eventually leads to stagflation fears, yielding negative 60-day forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns in stagflationary periods do not differ significantly from baseline, or the hypothesis predicts positive returns when CPIAUCSL and UNRATE both rise.
